id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0001
text: # Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0001
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

text: [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0003

metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

text: [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0004
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0005
text: [Mario Bersem](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/mario-bersem/index.html)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0005
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0006
text: Restrictions on the use of privacy-sensitive information are turning the online advertising market upside down. Advertisers, publishers and agencies are looking for alternatives to today’s personalisation practice.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0006
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0007
text: (Can’t see a download or unable to download the report? Then upgrade your browser to the most recent version.)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0007
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0008
text: - [Privacy protection puts the online advertising market at a turning point (December 2022)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0008
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0009
text: 2 MB](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/bescherming-privacy-zet-markt-voor-online-adverteren-op-keerpunt_tcm16-179811.pdf)

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0009
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0010
text: Based on a survey of 544 industry respondents and in-depth interviews with experts, ABN AMRO sets out a vision for the future of online advertising. Advertisers, publishers and agencies are exploring alternatives to the current personalisation practice, such as contextual advertising, generating their own data and more collaboration on data. Professionals in the online advertising chain also think the big tech players will come up with new solutions and so manage to retain their power.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0010
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0011
text: Person-specific – also known as personalised – ads have become the norm, at least online; by some estimates, a form of personalisation is used for 80 per cent of online ads.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0011
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0012
text: But this personalisation practice is now under heavy pressure. Europe is trying, with new laws and regulations, to curb the personalisation of ads and even ban it for minors. The new rules, mainly set out in the Digital Services Act (DSA) which took effect on 16 November 2022, are driven by the desire to protect users’ privacy, especially because of the unbridled trade in personal data that lies behind personalised ads. In the words of MEP Paul Tang: “The time when personal data are freely collected and traded for advertising purposes is coming to an end.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0012
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0013
text: At the same time, the big tech companies also seem to have heard their users’ call for privacy. On Apple devices, users can now choose to switch off tracking, and Alphabet has announced that in 2024 it will stop supporting third-party cookies in Google Chrome, i.e. by far the world’s most used web browser will stop supporting the most used tracking method. Regulation and market forces are therefore pointing in the same direction: an online advertising market that is less dependent on privacy-sensitive data and where personal data are collected with the conscious and explicit consent of the end user.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0013
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0014
text: ABN AMRO research shows that as many as one third of the professionals surveyed who work at advertisers and publishers consider their organisation dependent on personalised online ads. Contextual advertising, where only the content of a web page or, for example, the time of day determines the specific ad, can offer them a solution, as various examples show. As an alternative, advertisers and publishers can develop a data strategy – separately or possibly together – that relies on personal profiles created with informed consent and that the person in question can also view and adjust. In other words: conscious and explicit consent that can also be withdrawn. A majority of respondents indicate they do not yet have enough of their own data to offer personalised ads.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0014
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0015
text: An in-house data strategy is not expected to be feasible for all advertisers and publishers, due to the costs, compliance risks and required scale involved. This is one reason why the advertising giants, as now, will continue to offer solutions to enable personalised advertising, although these will be based on less privacy-sensitive data and will no longer target minors, at least in Europe.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0015
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0016
text: The online advertising market is at a turning point. Because of increasing restrictions on collecting and using personal data, publishers, advertisers and agencies are forced to rethink their strategy. At the same time, the major technology companies will do everything they can to retain their power. In this report, ABN AMRO sets out a vision for the future based on a survey conducted with PanelWizard and in-depth interviews with experts from the online advertising industry.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0016
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0017
text: In 2021, people spent on average about [seven hours](https://datareportal.com/reports/digital-2022-october-global-statshot "DIGITAL 2022: OCTOBER GLOBAL STATSHOT REPORT") a day in online channels. Advertisers therefore spend billions a year to show their message to website visitors and app users. This market is growing fast; in 2021 this amount in Europe had risen to [92 billion euros](https://iabeurope.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/adex-2021-advertising-report.pdf "DIGITAL ADVERTISING IN EUROPE") – an increase of 30.5 per cent compared with the previous year.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0017
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0018
text: In the Netherlands, digital ad spend exceeded advertising spend in traditional media for the first time in 2016 and by now the digital ad spend is already more than twice as large. For the owners of websites, apps and other content – also known as ‘publishers’ – the internet offers opportunities to monetise their ad space. For example, almost half of the largest websites use a so-called ad network. This is a technological solution that collects online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0018
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0019
text: Besides advertisers and publishers, there are numerous intermediaries and other service providers active in the online advertising market. Advertisers are supported by media, marketing and advertising agencies, which help determine the most effective channels for online ad campaigns and often also play a role in the associated purchase of ad space. There are also numerous so-called AdTech players, which use technological solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of online ad space.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0019
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0020
text: This process is in most cases fully automated and works by means of an auction – also called Real Time Bidding (RTB). When someone visits a specific part of a website or app, a technological solution on the advertiser’s side determines whether to bid for the attention of the person in question. This is based on predefined criteria, which often relate to the individual – for example ‘woman’, ‘living in South Holland’, ‘interested in pregnancy and babies’. Large amounts of data on internet users are used to determine the best-fitting ad for each individual. Such data often come from various sources: from visited websites and downloaded apps to search terms used and videos watched.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0020
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0021
text: Professionals working in the online advertising chain are generally positive about this practice, according to a survey by ABN AMRO in collaboration with PanelWizard. Of the 544 respondents, two thirds (67 per cent) are employed by an advertiser; the rest work at a publisher (19 per cent) or agency (13 per cent).

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0021
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0022
text: More than half of advertisers and a large majority of agencies indicate that personalised ads are an effective way to reach their target audience. Two thirds of publishers believe it is an effective way to monetise their ad space. Dependence on personalised ads appears substantial on both sides; about one third of advertisers (31 per cent) and publishers (34 per cent), and even forty per cent of agencies say they are dependent on this form of targeting.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0022
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0023
text: “Personalisation is important,” says Rogier Hosman, Chief Marketing Officer at digital agency Youwe. The company helps businesses develop and implement a digital strategy, including in online marketing. “Companies have a whole range of segments and customer types, all with different needs. So advertisers need to serve the right story to the right person.” According to the marketing boss, personalisation is also in the interest of the person who sees the ad. “As a consumer, it is pleasant to receive a relevant message and not have to wade through all kinds of nonsensical information.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0023
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0024
text: However, the current online advertising market focused on personalisation is changing for several reasons in favour of a – possibly – more privacy-friendly model. New laws and regulations play a decisive role here. For example, the European Digital Services Act (DSA) prohibits ads based on sensitive data such as sexual orientation and political preference. No personalised ads may be shown to minors at all. For adult internet users, it becomes easier to say “no” to tracking. The sister law, the Digital Markets Act (DMA), also raises various barriers.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0024
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0025
text: Since most privacy-restricting legislation is still recent, the effects are only becoming clear over time. For example, according to civil rights groups and privacy watchdogs, it is questionable whether the RTB system complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) of 2018. Consent by the internet user must, among other things, be informed, which means that in advance it is clear who will process the data, for what purpose and in what way. Due to the complexity of the auction system and the large number of parties with whom personal data are shared, informed consent in the context of current online ad auctions is, according to critics, not feasible in advance.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0025
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0026
text: Legislation is seen as the main growth barrier for the market for personalised ads, the survey shows. Growing consumer awareness of their privacy and rights is, according to respondents, the second most important barrier. That internet users do indeed make use of the option to refuse personalised ads if that option is accessible enough was experienced by Nederlandse Publieke Omroep (NPO). When the broadcaster introduced a new type of pop-up that made both refusal of and consent to personalised ads possible with one click, nine out of ten website visitors did not agree. iPhone users have also overwhelmingly chosen not to be tracked since this became possible; 75 per cent of tracking requests by apps were answered with a “no” in early 2022.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0026
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0027
text: A related development at the expense of personalised advertising is the fact that the third-party cookie, the dominant technology for tracking internet users, will soon no longer be supported by the largest web browsers. After Safari – found on Apple devices – and Firefox, Google Chrome will also stop supporting this tracking vehicle from 2024. Although around eighty per cent of online ads are currently personalised based on information collected via third-party cookies, the professionals surveyed do not seem alarmed. A small minority indicate they are not well prepared for a future without third-party cookies.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0027
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0028
text: This interplay between binding laws and explicit consumer preferences may lead to a larger number of refusals to use personal data for advertising purposes. Combined with the disappearance of the third-party cookie, it becomes more difficult to create a user profile based on previously visited websites and behaviour on those websites. In the end, less data will be available to tell an advertiser or intermediary what kind of visitor they are dealing with. A more privacy-friendly advertising model therefore not only fits the spirit of the times, it is also the only option.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0028
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0029
text: Changing social standards and associated laws and regulations are putting various principles of the online advertising market under pressure. Full transparency and control over the use of personal data will become the new standard, and contextual advertising will gain – or rather regain – ground due to new restrictions, given its history in traditional media.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0029
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0030
text: Big tech remains dominant, according to the consensus among respondents. A large majority (77 per cent) expect that the share of ad spend flowing to these parties will be the same or higher in five years’ time (currently the share is more than half, see box D on page 21). Google has a solid position due to its activities across the entire AdTech chain (see box A). The channels of both Google and Meta are also particularly attractive to advertisers due to their extensive data collection. For example, Google knows perfectly what someone’s interests are based on their search behaviour, and social medium Instagram has insight into users through likes, posts and followed accounts. Based on such data, targeted ads can be shown.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0030
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0031
text: Most other websites, unlike such walled gardens, are not autonomously able to collect so many insights about their users. That is why personalised ads at smaller publishers are often created based on third-party data – including browsing behaviour on previously visited websites. The use of such tracking data will soon be made more difficult by the DSA. “Truly personalised advertising will be confined within the walled gardens,” expects Havik of DPG Media.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0031
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0032
text: Google will continue to play an important role for advertisers and publishers, expects Havik of DPG Media. This mainly concerns small publishers, whose potential reach and the amount of available visitor data are both insufficient to interest advertisers one-to-one. That is why small publishers rely heavily on platforms that bring together supply of and demand for ad space. Google is the market leader in this.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0032
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0033
text: According to Havik, smaller publishers must accept that dependence, or revert to the traditional subscription model. “They can choose to consider their ad revenue lost and derive their income from the consumer market. Through a paywall they can then have visitors pay for access to articles.” JustPremium co-founder Visser believes the earning potential from advertising is limited anyway for websites with relatively few visitors. “If you don’t have serious scale, you can’t earn serious money with online ads,” he says. “People no longer browse endlessly, but go purposefully to a website where they know exactly what they can get. For example, reading news or buying a product.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0033
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0034
text: In addition to the power of large technology companies, the will of the consumer also determines which way the online advertising market ultimately goes. Their privacy awareness will likely be further strengthened in the coming years by stricter enforcement of the European privacy law GDPR and the requirements of the Digital Services Act. The latter requires clear communication about data use for online ads and makes it easier to say “no” to tracking. Both requirements put the power – or at least part of it – back in the hands of the internet user.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0034
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0035
text: Almost one third of respondents (30 per cent) expect to see an increased willingness among consumers in five years’ time to share their data for advertising purposes; a slightly higher percentage (35 per cent) expect a decrease. Transparency is in any case essential to be able to continue offering personalised ads in the future, thinks Hosman of Youwe Digital Agency. “In a few years, internet users will no longer accept that they have to read somewhere in a sub-sub-agreement how their data are used exactly,” he says. “We should not underestimate the power and will of consumers. Otherwise you will see the tide turn.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0035
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0036
text: DPG Media is developing an online environment where every reader can change their user settings. There people will soon also be able to indicate how their data may be used for advertising purposes, says Havik. “For everything we do in the field of advertising, the rule is: you must be able to explain it to your mother. And she must also agree with it.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0036
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0037
text: Although personal data have in recent years become the main fuel in the online advertising world, creativity also has a clear role. An ad must also be visually appealing, Visser notes. “A large part of a campaign’s success can be traced back to the creative,” he says. The entrepreneur refers to the combination of image and text of an ad, an area where advertising agencies have proven their added value for many years. “Data are much less important than was long thought.” Kroontje of Pangaea Digital Agency agrees that reduced availability of personal data does not necessarily undermine an ad strategy. “Most of the result comes from traditional marketing. That starts with a good understanding of your target audience.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0037
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0038
text: More than sixty per cent of respondents believe that the importance of creativity will have somewhat or even strongly increased in five years’ time. This applies even more to agencies: as many as 75 per cent expect an increase. It seems they are most clearly preparing for a future in which the boundless availability of personal data is not a given.

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0038
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0039
text: The market is not moving towards one specific strategy, as the survey on solutions already showed. This is also reflected in the respondents’ vision for the future, who can imagine a growing role for both contextual and personalised advertising. More than half expect a higher degree of personalisation of online ads in five years’ time, despite legal restrictions. This matches the vision of the future held by Havik of DPG Media: “The principle of value exchange – in this case good content in return for data – will continue to exist on websites that know their own visitors well.” Visser takes the same view. “Within walled gardens, there will still be endless personalisation in future. But,” he qualifies, “outside them, contextual advertising will gain ground, or personalisation will take place at a less granular level – such as with Google Topics.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0039
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0040
text: The future is in any case not black and white, Kroontje of Pangaea also thinks. The entrepreneur expects that information about individuals will continue to play a role in the context of online advertising. “I believe there will be a balance. If you know nothing about someone, then you are sailing blind. You actually go back to TV advertising in terms of the level of personalisation. I don’t expect that to happen.” Kroontje stresses that personalisation of ads does not necessarily equal online tracking. “If you can indicate your own preferences and interests, many users will not mind. For example, I don’t like football but I do like golf. If I can tick that I only want to see ads about golf, then that’s only good.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0040
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0041
text: According to Hosman of Youwe Digital Agency, it is important for marketers to keep an open mind at least. “As a marketing team, you should not become addicted to one solution. Current developments in the market are leading to new solutions, each of which is worth considering.”

metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0041
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__c4-paragraph__0042
text: Read the full report ‘Privacy protection puts the online advertising market at a turning point’ for solutions in a changing playing field.
